output.final_deliverable:
Chain of Wikipedia pages (each next page linked from the previous):

1) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
2) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents
3) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Mathematics
4) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mathematics
5) https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclid

Verified adjacency (showing that page 4 links to page 5):
- From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mathematics the page contains a link to Euclid: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclid (verified on the visited Mathematics page) [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mathematics].

Sources (visited pages):
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Contents
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portal:Mathematics
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mathematics
- https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euclid
output.action_summary: I started at Wikipedia Main Page and followed links found on each page to build a 5-page chain: Main Page → Portal:Contents → Portal:Mathematics → Mathematics → Euclid. I verified the adjacency from the Mathematics page to the Euclid page by confirming the Euclid link on the Mathematics page (see visited Mathematics URL). All pages visited are Wikipedia URLs cited above.

317990e6-2509-4cc9-b261-496919e0fcdc.title:
Start at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and build a chain of at least 4 Wikipedia URLs. Each next URL must come from links found on the previous visited page. Return the chain in order, and include one verified adjacency pair near the end (show that URL[i] links to URL[i+1]).

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.
